shortDescription.text: Server-Side Request Forgery via user-controlled URL in needle.get()
Reflected XSS via unsanitized HTTP response body written directly to client
Open redirect via unvalidated user-supplied URL in query parameter
Known prototype pollution vulnerability in needle 2.2.4 dependency

message.text: The URL passed to needle.get() is constructed entirely from user-controlled query parameters (req.query.url + req.query.symbol). An attacker can set 'url' to an internal service address (e.g., http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/) to reach internal infrastructure, read cloud metadata, or scan internal networks. No validation, allowlisting, or URL parsing is applied to the input.

The response body from the external needle.get() request is written directly to the HTTP response via res.write(body) without any sanitization or encoding. If the remote server (or an attacker-controlled server via SSRF) returns HTML/JavaScript content, it will be rendered in the victim's browser as the Content-Type is set to text/html. This is a reflected XSS vulnerability chained with the SSRF.

The /learn route redirects to a URL taken directly from req.query.url without any validation. An attacker can craft a link such as /learn?url=https://evil.com to redirect authenticated users to a phishing site. No allowlist or domain check is applied.

needle 2.2.4 is affected by CVE-2020-7669 (prototype pollution via crafted response headers) and related vulnerabilities. If an attacker controls the target server (possible via the SSRF), malicious response headers can pollute Object.prototype, potentially leading to denial of service or remote code execution depending on downstream usage.
